disclaimer: Seed data compiled from public sources. Every row confirmed_by_counsel=false until ratified by immigration counsel. Not legal advice.

rules.value: 1 full academic year of lawful full-time enrollment at an SEVP-certified school
rules.notes: Applies to both CPT and OPT. Time in a study abroad program counts if the student first spent at least one full academic term enrolled full-time in the US. Longstanding provision; effective date shown is publication of the 1992 practical training restructuring (57 FR 31954). Verified in current eCFR text as of 2026-07-01.

rules.value: Students in English language training programs are ineligible for practical training (CPT and OPT)
rules.notes: Verified in current eCFR text as of 2026-07-01.
F-1 student may be admitted up to 30 days before the report/program start date on the Form I-20. Admission is for duration of status (D/S) as of 2026-07-06; see pending-fixed-admission-period-rule.
One-time I-901 SEVIS fee for F-1/F-3 students; required per 8 CFR 214.2(f)(19). No change identified through 2026-07-06.

rules.value: Graduate students whose program requires immediate participation in curricular practical training are exempt from the one-academic-year requirement
rules.notes: Regulatory text: 'Exceptions to the one academic year requirement are provided for students enrolled in graduate studies that require immediate participation in curricular practical training.'

rules.value: DSO authorizes CPT in SEVIS and endorses the Form I-20; no USCIS filing, no fee, and no EAD required
rules.notes: 274a.12(b)(6)(iii): CPT (part-time or full-time) 'is authorized by the Designated School Official on the student's Form I-20. No Service endorsement is necessary.' DSO records employer, location, full-time/part-time, and start/end dates in SEVIS.

rules.value: CPT must be an integral part of an established curriculum: alternative work/study, internship, cooperative education, or other required internship/practicum offered by sponsoring employers through cooperative agreements with the school; must be directly related to the major area of study
rules.notes: Verified in current eCFR text as of 2026-07-01.

rules.value: Student may begin CPT only after receiving the Form I-20 with the DSO endorsement (DSO must sign, date, and return the I-20 before employment commences)
rules.notes: CPT is employer-, location-, and date-specific as recorded in SEVIS.

SEVP guidance: 20 hours or less per week is part-time CPT; more than 20 hours is full-time CPT. Verified directly from the SEVIS Help Hub CPT page (fetched 2026-07-06): 'Full Time: More than 20 hours per week. Part Time: 20 hours or less per week.' The 20-hour threshold is not stated in 8 CFR 214.2(f)(10)(i) for CPT; it parallels the pre-completion OPT threshold in (f)(10)(ii)(A)(2). Guidance-level value, not regulation.

Exact regulatory text: 'Students who have received one year or more of full time curricular practical training are ineligible for post-completion academic training.' DHS/USCIS interpret this as ineligibility for post-completion OPT at that degree level. Applies at 12 or more months aggregate of FULL-TIME CPT; CPT under 12 months does not reduce the OPT allotment.

rules.source_citation: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 2, Part F, Ch. 5 (Practical Training); 8 CFR 214.2(f)(10)(i)
rules.notes: Part-time CPT (20 hours/week or less), and full-time CPT totaling less than 12 months, do not count against OPT eligibility. Only 12+ months of full-time CPT eliminates OPT at that degree level. Verified 2026-07-06 directly from the SEVIS Help Hub OPT page, which conditions OPT ineligibility on 12 months or more of FULL-TIME CPT only ('The student has not been authorized for 12 months or more of full-time Curricular Practical Training (CPT)'; 'One year of full-time CPT eliminates a student's eligibility for OPT'). Full-time CPT at a previous school at the same program level counts toward the 12-month total; CPT at another program level does not affect OPT.

12 months of practical training per eligibility; shared allotment between pre-completion and post-completion OPT. Regulatory text: 'A student may be authorized 12 months of practical training, and becomes eligible for another 12 months of practical training when they change to a higher educational level.'

A student becomes eligible for another 12 months of OPT only upon changing to a HIGHER educational level (e.g., bachelor's to master's). A second degree at the same level does not confer a new allotment.

rules.value: Employment must be directly related to the student's major area of study
rules.notes: Students must be able to demonstrate the direct relationship; SEVP requires reporting how employment relates to the degree.
Hours per week. Pre-completion OPT may not exceed 20 hours/week while school is in session; may be full-time during annual vacation and when school is not in session.

rules.source_citation: USCIS OPT for F-1 Students page; USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 2, Part F, Ch. 5
rules.notes: Part-time pre-completion OPT is deducted from the 12-month allotment at one-half the full-time rate (e.g., 1 year part-time pre-completion OPT leaves 6 months post-completion OPT). Full-time pre-completion OPT is deducted 1:1. Deduction rate is USCIS policy, not express regulatory text. Verified 2026-07-06 directly from the USCIS 'Optional Practical Training (OPT) for F-1 Students' page: 1 year of part-time (20 hours per week) pre-completion OPT reduces available full-time OPT 'by 6 months, 50% of the previously authorized year at the same education level.'
Pre-completion OPT I-765 may be filed up to 90 days before completing one full academic year, provided employment will not start before the academic year is completed.
Post-completion OPT Form I-765 may be properly filed up to 90 days before the program end date.
Filing no later than 60 days after the program end date (i.e., during the 60-day grace period).
The I-765 must be properly filed with (received by) USCIS within 30 days of the date the DSO enters the OPT recommendation into SEVIS. Late filing is a common denial ground.
A student may not request an OPT start date more than 60 days after the program end date. Employment authorization begins on the requested date or the adjudication date, whichever is later.

rules.value: Employment may not begin before the start date on the approved EAD (Form I-766)
rules.notes: Sole exception is the automatic extension while a timely-filed STEM OPT extension is pending (8 CFR 274a.12(b)(6)(iv)).
All post-completion OPT must be completed within 14 months following completion of study, except the 24-month STEM extension need not be completed within that window.

Aggregate calendar days of unemployment during any post-completion OPT period (8 CFR 274a.12(c)(3)(i)(B)). The period begins on the approved EAD start date. F-1 status during post-completion OPT is dependent upon employment.

rules.source_citation: SEVP Policy Guidance 1004-03 (Apr. 23, 2010), sec. 7.1.8; SEVIS Help Hub
rules.notes: Time abroad while unemployed counts toward the unemployment limit, unless the student is on employer-authorized leave or traveling as part of employment. Verified 2026-07-06 directly from SEVP Policy Guidance 1004-03 sec. 7.1.8 (PDF fetched from ice.gov): time spent outside the U.S. during an approved period of post-completion OPT 'counts as unemployment' unless the student is employed during employer-authorized leave or traveling as part of employment. SEVP guidance, not express regulatory text; the 2010 guidance references the then-current 90/120-day limits (now 90/150 under the 2016 rule).

rules.source_citation: SEVP OPT Policy Guidance 1004-03; SEVIS Help Hub
rules.notes: Hours per week. Verified 2026-07-06 directly from SEVP Policy Guidance 1004-03 sec. 7.1.5 ('A student must work at least 20 hours per week in a qualifying position to be considered employed'; variable schedules must average at least 20 hours/week within a month) and the SEVIS Help Hub OPT page (post-completion OPT 'must be at least 20 hours per week or full-time'). For regular (non-STEM) post-completion OPT, unpaid/volunteer positions of 20+ hours/week that do not violate labor laws qualify (Guidance 1004-03, 'Unpaid employment'). Guidance-level rule, not regulation.

rules.value: OPT employment authorization automatically terminates when the student transfers to another school or begins study at another educational level
rules.notes: Also automatically suspended during certified strike/labor dispute at the place of employment (8 CFR 214.2(f)(14)).
Duration of status for a student on post-completion OPT runs from proper filing of the OPT application through the authorized OPT period and ends 60 days after the OPT EAD expires.

60-day period to prepare for departure, transfer, change educational level, or file a change of status after completing the course of study and any authorized practical training. Applies both after program completion (f1_studying) and after OPT/STEM OPT EAD expiry. No employment permitted during the grace period. IN FORCE as of 2026-07-06; the pending fixed-admission-period rule would cut this to 30 days if finalized (see pending-fixed-admission-period-rule).
15-day departure period when the DSO authorizes withdrawal from classes. No grace period for unauthorized withdrawal or other failure to maintain status.

rules.value: Student on OPT must report any change of name or address, and any interruption of employment, to the DSO for the duration of OPT; DSO maintains the SEVIS record
rules.notes: In practice reported via the SEVP Portal and/or the DSO. Employer name/address and how employment relates to the degree are reported in SEVIS.

All F-1 students (including those on OPT) must report legal name or address changes within 10 days; DSO enters in SEVIS within 21 days. Effective date approximate (SEVIS-era codification); provision longstanding.
Student on post-completion OPT returning from temporary travel abroad needs an unexpired EAD plus a Form I-20 endorsed for reentry by the DSO within the last 6 months.

rules.value: (c)(3)(A) pre-completion OPT; (c)(3)(B) post-completion OPT; (c)(3)(C) 24-month STEM extension
rules.notes: Category codes used on Form I-765.

Online filing fee for Form I-765. No separate biometrics fee. Unchanged as of the G-1055 fee schedule edition dated 2026-05-29 (verified 2026-07-06 directly from the G-1055 PDF: 'Paper Filing: $520 Online Filing: $470'). Same fee applies to the STEM extension I-765.
Paper filing fee for Form I-765. Unchanged as of the G-1055 fee schedule edition dated 2026-05-29 (verified 2026-07-06 directly from the G-1055 PDF).
SUPERSEDED. Premium processing fee for Form I-765 categories (c)(3) (F-1 OPT/STEM OPT) from 2024-02-26 through 2026-02-28.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 106.4; 91 FR 1059 (Jan. 12, 2026), DHS final rule 'Adjustment to Premium Processing Fees'
rules.notes: CURRENT. Applies to premium processing requests postmarked on or after 2026-03-01 for I-765 (c)(3) F-1 OPT and STEM OPT applications. CPI-U inflation adjustment (+5.72% June 2023 to June 2025). Verified 2026-07-06 against the Federal Register API (91 FR 1059, effective 2026-03-01), the rule's full text, and USCIS G-1055 fee schedule (ed. 2026-05-29), which lists $1,780 for I-765 OPT and STEM-OPT premium processing.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 106.4; USCIS premium processing expansion for F-1 categories (Mar. 2023)
rules.notes: BUSINESS days, not calendar days. Premium processing available for I-765 categories (c)(3)(A), (c)(3)(B), (c)(3)(C). The 30-business-day timeframe verified 2026-07-06 in current 8 CFR 106.4 via eCFR ('Application for employment authorization: 30 business days'); a business day excludes weekends and federally observed holidays. Effective date reflects USCIS's March 2023 premium processing expansion to F-1 categories (announcement-based, secondary corroboration only).

SUPERSEDED (historical lineage). The 2008 17-month STEM extension was vacated in Washington Alliance of Technology Workers v. DHS and replaced by the 24-month rule effective 2016-05-10. A student granted a 17-month extension counts as having used one lifetime STEM extension (8 CFR 214.2(f)(10)(ii)(C)).

CURRENT. 24-month extension of post-completion OPT for qualifying STEM degree holders. Combined with 12-month OPT gives up to 36 months of work authorization per qualifying degree cycle. Rule upheld; WashTech litigation ended with cert denied Oct. 2023.

'In no event may a student be authorized for more than two lifetime STEM OPT extensions.' One extension per qualifying degree; a second extension requires a new qualifying STEM degree at a HIGHER degree level and a new period of post-completion OPT. A prior 17-month extension counts as one use.

rules.value: Bachelor's, master's, or doctoral degree from a U.S. institution accredited by a Department of Education-recognized agency and SEVP-certified, in a CIP category on the DHS STEM Designated Degree Program List at the time of the DSO recommendation
rules.notes: STEM fields defined via the Department of Education CIP taxonomy (engineering, biological sciences, mathematics, physical sciences, and related fields). List published on the SEVP website (ice.gov/sevis) and updated via Federal Register notices.

Most recent list update as of 2026-07-06: added Environmental/Natural Resource Economics (CIP 03.0204). Re-verified 2026-07-06 via the Federal Register API: 89 FR 59748 (Notice, published 2024-07-23) confirmed, and no subsequent STEM list update published through 2026-07-06. Current list at studyinthestates.dhs.gov/stem-opt-hub.

A STEM extension may be based on a previously earned qualifying STEM degree (not the degree underlying the current OPT), provided it was conferred by an accredited, SEVP-certified U.S. institution within the 10 years preceding the DSO recommendation and is on the current STEM list. The training must be directly related to the qualifying degree.

Employer must be enrolled in E-Verify (valid Company ID or Client Company ID), remain a participant in good standing, and have an EIN. E-Verify requirement dates to the 2008 17-month rule; carried into the 2016 24-month rule.

Individualized Form I-983 signed by student and employer official with signatory authority, submitted to the DSO BEFORE the DSO may recommend the extension in SEVIS. Must state training goals, evaluation process, oversight/supervision, and direct relationship to the qualifying STEM degree; student must disclose compensation.
STEM extension I-765 may be filed up to 90 days before the current post-completion OPT EAD expires, and must be filed while the student is in a valid period of post-completion OPT.
The STEM extension I-765 must be properly filed with USCIS within 60 days of the date the DSO enters the extension recommendation into SEVIS (versus 30 days for initial post-completion OPT).

If the STEM extension I-765 is timely filed and the OPT EAD expires while it is pending, employment authorization and the EAD are automatically extended from the EAD expiration date until USCIS decides the application, up to 180 days. Proof: expired EAD plus I-20 endorsed by the DSO recommending the extension.

rules.value: STEM extension employment authorization begins the day after the initial post-completion OPT EAD expires and ends 24 months later, regardless of the date the extension is approved
rules.notes: No gap and no tolling: late adjudication does not shift the 24-month window.

Aggregate cap of 150 days across the TOTAL OPT period (initial post-completion OPT plus the 24-month extension). INCLUSIVE of unemployment days accrued during initial OPT - the counter does not reset (i.e., an additional 60 days on top of the 90-day initial-OPT limit). Under the superseded 17-month rule the total was 120 days.

Hours per week. 'A student may not engage in practical training for less than 20 hours per week,' excluding leave consistent with policies for similarly situated U.S. workers. STEM OPT training must be paid employment with the I-983 employer (volunteering does not qualify, unlike regular OPT).

rules.value: Duties, hours, and compensation must be commensurate with terms and conditions applicable to the employer's similarly situated U.S. workers in the area of employment
rules.notes: Compensation (including adjustments) must be disclosed on the Form I-983. Applies even if the employer has fewer than three similarly situated U.S. workers.

rules.value: Employer attests on Form I-983 that it has sufficient resources and personnel to provide the training, that the student will not replace a full- or part-time, temporary or permanent U.S. worker, and that the opportunity helps the student attain training goals
rules.notes: Non-displacement attestation is a compliance obligation the platform should track per employer.

BUSINESS days. Employer must report the student's termination or departure to the DSO within 5 business days. A student is deemed departed when the employer knows the student left, or after 5 consecutive business days of unexcused absence, whichever is earlier.

If the student starts a new practical training opportunity with a new employer during the 24-month extension, a new Form I-983 must be submitted to the DSO within 10 days of beginning it, followed by a new DSO recommendation. New employer must also be E-Verify enrolled.

rules.value: Student and employer must sign a modified Form I-983 reflecting any material change to or deviation from the training plan and submit it to the DSO at the earliest available opportunity
rules.notes: CORRECTION to common summaries: the regulatory standard for modified I-983 submission is 'earliest available opportunity,' not a fixed 10-day deadline. Material changes include EIN change from restructuring, non-hours-based compensation reduction, significant decrease in hours, hours dropping below the 20/week minimum, or anything rendering an employer attestation inaccurate. (The 10-day deadlines apply to student change-reporting under (f)(12)(ii)(A) and new-employer I-983s under (f)(10)(ii)(C)(7)(iv).)

Within 10 days of the change, a STEM OPT student must report to the DSO any change of legal name, residential or mailing address, employer name, employer address, and/or loss of employment. Compliance is required to maintain F-1 status.

Six-month validation report confirming no change to name/address/employer/employment status, i.e., at 6, 12, 18, and 24 months from the start of the 24-month extension. Continues until F-1 status expires or the extension concludes, whichever is first.
BUSINESS days. Each validation report is due to the DSO within 10 business days of the reporting date.

rules.value: Initial self-evaluation within 12 months of the STEM EAD start date, plus a concluding evaluation; each signed by student and employer, and delivered to the DSO no later than 10 days after the end of the reporting period or conclusion of training
rules.notes: These I-983 self-evaluations (12-month and final/24-month) are distinct from the six-month SEVIS validation reports under (f)(12)(ii)(B).
Value is in HOURS. DHS may conduct employer site visits with 48 hours advance notice, except no notice is required when the visit is triggered by a complaint or other evidence of noncompliance.

School must ensure SEVP access to each I-983 and associated evaluations beginning within 30 days of submission to the DSO and continuing for 3 years after completion of each STEM training opportunity.

rules.value: October 1 of the fiscal year for which H-1B status is requested
rules.notes: SUPERSEDED. From 2008 through 2025-01-16, the automatic cap-gap extension of F-1 status and OPT/STEM OPT work authorization ran only until October 1.

rules.value: April 1 of the fiscal year for which H-1B status is requested, or the validity start date of the approved petition, whichever is earlier
rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)(A); 89 FR 103054 (Dec. 18, 2024), H-1B modernization final rule
rules.notes: CURRENT. Verified in eCFR text as of 2026-07-01. Duration of status AND employment authorization granted under 8 CFR 274a.12(c)(3)(i)(B) or (C) (post-completion OPT and STEM OPT) are automatically extended for beneficiaries of timely filed, nonfrivolous cap-subject H-1B change-of-status petitions requesting a start date in that fiscal year. Applies beginning with FY 2026 cap filings (April 2025). Also applies from f1_stem_opt.

rules.value: Automatic extension terminates upon rejection, denial, revocation, or withdrawal of the H-1B petition, or upon denial or withdrawal of the change-of-status request (even if the petition is approved for consular processing); student must not have violated status
rules.notes: Work authorization during cap-gap per 8 CFR 274a.12(b)(6)(v). Renamed from capgap-termination-events to avoid rule_id collision with rules_capgap.json.
The automatic extension of duration of status also applies to F-2 dependents (status only, not work authorization).

rules.value: DHS rule 'Establishing a Fixed Time Period of Admission and an Extension of Stay Procedure for Nonimmigrant Academic Students, Exchange Visitors, and Representatives of Foreign Information Media' would replace duration of status (D/S) with fixed admission up to 4 years, cut the 60-day grace period to 30 days, and require I-539 extension-of-stay filings to cover OPT/STEM OPT periods beyond the admitted period. NOT IN FORCE as of 2026-07-06.
rules.source_citation: NPRM published Aug. 28, 2025 (FR Doc. 2025-16554); final rule cleared OMB/OIRA review 2026-06-17, not yet published
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: the OIRA review conclusion date (2026-06-17, disposition 'Consistent with Change') and the delayed effective date (NPRM proposed 60 days after publication; some trackers say 30-60 days) come from NAFSA/law-firm regulatory trackers, not a primary Federal Register document (none exists yet). Independently re-verified 2026-07-06 via the Federal Register API: NO final rule establishing a fixed time period of admission has been published, so D/S, the 60-day grace period, and no-I-539-for-OPT all remain in force. Re-verify on publication and watch for litigation.
